title: registry/ + defaults/ dirs retained on disk as AJV test fixtures after consumption-MVP de-canonicalized them; delete pending fixture-repoint + a home for 8 conception-less legacy schemas
description: The consumption MVP (TASK-055) made samples/ the single canonical catalog: installProject + initProject + generate-skills now read samples/, and registry/ + defaults/ were dropped from the shipped package.json files[] (DEC-0011). But the two dirs were NOT deleted from disk because (a) ~7 test files read packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/*.json directly as AJV fixtures (schema-validator.test, context-contracts.test, execution-context.integration.test, index.test + pi-workflows macros/render-architecture/render-task tests) and (b) 8 schemas live ONLY in registry/ with no samples/ home (architecture, audit, conformance-reference, domain, handoff, plan, project, roadmap — legacy, absent from the going-forward DEC-0037 conception). So registry/ + defaults/ are now dead-but-present: unshipped, unread by production, retained solely as test fixtures + legacy-schema holding.

evidence.reference: reads registry/schemas as AJV fixtures (one of ~7 test readers); deletion would break these
files[] no longer lists registry/ or defaults/ (TASK-055 commit a53663e) — dirs ship nowhere but remain in the repo tree

impact: Dead dirs in the repo tree are misleading (a reader may assume registry/ is still canonical) and the legacy-fixture coupling blocks a clean cutover. Low-urgency (no production path touches them; samples/ is canonical) hence P2. Becomes relevant at the .context cutover (DEC-0036) when the substrate vocabulary is finalized.
proposed_resolution: Repoint the ~7 registry-fixture tests to samples/schemas (where the kind exists) or to inline fixtures; decide each of the 8 conception-less legacy schemas — either give it a samples/ home (if still wanted in the going-forward conception) or drop it (legacy planning vocab not in DEC-0037). Then delete packages/pi-context/registry/ + packages/pi-context/defaults/. Sequence with the .context cutover; not blocking.
